onDismiss.description: Callback when toast should be dismissed

duration.description: Auto-dismiss duration in ms (0 = no auto-dismiss)

onPause.description: Callback when auto-dismiss timer should pause

onResume.description: Callback when auto-dismiss timer should resume

when: Non-critical information that doesn't require action
whenNot: Critical errors requiring user action (use Dialog)
guidelines: Use appropriate variant for the message type
Allow manual dismissal for longer messages
useToast() variant helpers accept either (title, description?) or an options object for action/duration/id
accessibility: Ensure sufficient display time for reading
Don't rely solely on color for meaning
Provide dismiss button with accessible label

examples.name: Default
examples.description: Interactive toast demo - click buttons to trigger toasts (includes helper object syntax example)

examples.name: Success
examples.code:
<Toast
  title="Success!"
  description="Your changes have been saved."
  variant="success"
/>

examples.name: Error
examples.code:
<Toast
  title="Error"
  description="Failed to save changes. Please try again."
  variant="error"
/>

examples.name: Warning
examples.code:
<Toast
  title="Warning"
  description="This action cannot be undone."
  variant="warning"
/>

examples.name: Info
examples.code:
<Toast
  title="New Update"
  description="Version 2.0 is now available."
  variant="info"
/>

examples.name: With Action
examples.code:
<Toast
  title="File deleted"
  description="The file has been moved to trash."
  action={{
    label: 'Undo',
    onClick: () => console.log('Undo clicked'),
  }}
/>

relations.note: Use Alert for persistent inline messages
Use Dialog for messages requiring user action

propsSummary: ToastInput (useToast): optional id for deterministic creation/replacement flows
useToast().success/error/warning/info: (title, description?) or ({ title, description, action, duration, id })
